title: President Trump Calls for Unity After Another Scary Incident
summary:
Have you ever been really scared? President Trump says he felt that way after someone tried to get close to him with guns and knives at a hotel in Washington, D.C. The man, Cole Tomas Allen, 31, from California, was stopped by security and taken to jail. This was the third time someone tried to hurt Trump in less than two years.

Trump looked serious and sad when he talked to reporters. He said, "It's always shocking when something like this happens." He called on all Americans—Republicans, Democrats, and everyone else—to stop fighting and come together. "We have to resolve our differences," he said.

But Trump has asked for unity before, and then quickly gone back to being combative (fighting with words). The next morning, he was already posting on social media about a new ballroom he's building at the White House. He said the attack "would never have happened" if the ballroom were finished.

Some people wonder if Trump will really change his tone this time. After a shooting at a rally in 2024, he gave a speech about healing, but soon after he was back to calling his opponents names. Let's see what happens next.
why_it_matters: This story shows that even leaders sometimes struggle to stay calm and work together, which is a lesson for how we treat each other in school and at home.

keywords.explanation: When people stop arguing and work together.
Something that surprises you in a bad way.
Wanting to argue or fight with words.
To find a solution to a problem.
People who are against you in an argument or election.
Getting better after something bad happens.

questions.question: Who was the suspect that tried to get close to Trump?

background_read: This was the third time someone tried to hurt Donald Trump (the U.S. president) in less than two years.
After a shooting at one of his rallies in 2024, Trump gave a speech about healing but soon returned to calling his opponents names.
Some people wonder if Trump will really stay calm and unify the country this time.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump and the suspect Cole Tomas Allen
WHAT: Another attempt on Trump's life at a hotel in Washington, D.C.; Trump called for unity.
WHERE: A hotel in Washington, D.C.
WHY: To urge Americans to stop fighting and resolve their differences.

perspectives.description: They see his call for unity as genuine and a step toward healing the country after violent threats.
They note that Trump has made similar calls before but quickly returned to combative behavior, making them doubt his sincerity.
They see this incident as a reminder of how political violence can hurt everyone, and how hard it is to keep a calm tone in a divided nation.
